purpose: Visual and editorial guardrails derived from official UK DRS guidance, Exchange for Change material and a review of public DRS imagery and retail return-point communication.
officialFacts: Launch date: 1 October 2027 for England, Scotland and Northern Ireland.
Eligible materials: single-use PET plastic bottles, aluminium cans and steel cans.
Customers pay a refundable deposit and reclaim it by returning an eligible empty container.
Exchange for Change is the Deposit Management Organisation for England, Scotland and Northern Ireland.
Wales must be treated as a separate scheme and not silently combined into Exchange for Change content.
visualPatterns: Show a simple circular journey: buy drink, pay deposit, return empty container, reclaim deposit, recycle into high-quality material.
Use recognisable PET bottle and metal can silhouettes rather than ambiguous packaging.
For consumer posts, show one clear return action and a visible, accessible inlet interaction.
For retailer posts, show realistic UK grocery, convenience-store or forecourt environments with practical floor-space context.
For public-sector posts, use system maps, material loops, collection-network diagrams and clean evidence-led infographic structures.
Use a single focal message, strong negative space and no more than three data cards.
Keep the approved ReteaRn UK RVM model visually exact whenever an RVM appears.
avoid: Irish Re-turn branding, Irish deposit values or retailer graphics presented as UK scheme facts.
Competitor logos or generic machines when the ReteaRn UK RVM is required.
Glass containers in Exchange for Change scheme graphics unless the post explicitly discusses Wales separately.
Overloaded collages, tiny legal text, decorative flags, illegible interface text or unsupported figures.
Calling the deposit a tax, fine, discount or guaranteed retailer profit.
